repairs: Block word-count, pace, and speech-window contradictions before dispatch
Carry a numeric terminal frame pad and reject windows that consume it
Carry a lip-sync confidence acceptance target alongside phoneme timing
Measure delayed speech onset in returned audio; prompt intent alone is not proof
Apply a 1.2x timing guard because declared provider pace is not exact runtime evidence
Reserve a 2.0-second post-phoneme settle window and forbid mouth reopening after speech
Separate the natural settle from a final 0.75-second open-eye boundary lock instead of freezing the face for two seconds
Never drop authored mix, vocal-signature, or micro-performance controls from the compact dispatch contract
claimBoundary: Each authenticated provider render is returned-media evidence for its exact packet only. The deterministic derivative is evidence for a local salvage workflow only. Neither establishes a permanent provider capability, universal framework quality, or fine lip-sync accuracy.
